characters.occupation_status: Home health aide until chapter_09; hospital transport by chapter_18.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads/writes English; speaks English and patois.

state_transitions.change: Loses the expected $5,800 partner draw.
Celia dies; Marcia loses her main job.
Flood sends her to New Testament Baptist shelter.
Signs the airport-area lease and starts hospital transport work.

characters.physical_details: Tall, thinning; bad right shoulder after chapter_10.

key_relationships.tiana_campbell: father who broke the paperwork promise

characters.occupation_status: Irregular handyman/day laborer; night maintenance by chapter_18.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads/writes English; speaks English and patois.

state_transitions.change: Borrows $400 from Claude Brown.
Admits he spent Tiana's renewal money on May rent.
Tears his right shoulder at Palm Court demolition.
Debt and Devon remittances are exposed; he leaves home.
Gets a key and partial return with a probationary night job.

characters.availability: Home through chapter_14; gone for several nights after the fight; back from chapter_15 on.

characters.occupation_status: Recent graduate; part-time worker; Miami Dade Honors enrollee by chapter_18.
characters.literacy_languages: Strong English reader/writer; speaks English with some home patois.

state_transitions.change: UF aid and housing stall over expired residency papers and missing 2017 parent verification.
Learns Delroy spent her renewal money; has sex with Malik for the first time.
Flood warps but does not destroy her key papers.
Misses the late UF check-in window while helping search for Micah; housing cancels.
Signs into Miami Dade Honors instead of Gainesville.

characters.physical_details: Lean, restless; cheek bruise after the police stop.

key_relationships.delroy_campbell: son drawn to him but afraid of becoming him

characters.occupation_status: Student on suspension and behavior probation; hidden dock and haircut cash.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads/writes English; speaks Miami teen English with patois exposure.

state_transitions.change: Gets ten days out-of-school suspension and semester probation.
Police stop leaves facial injuries; Marcia pays $90 for a clinic visit.
Shelter photo goes viral; he runs and is found at the old apartment.

characters.availability: Present except for the brief disappearance in chapter_17.

characters.occupation_status: Recent arrival from Jamaica doing salon/beauty cash work.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads/writes English; speaks English and patois.

state_transitions.change: Moves in with one suitcase, a folding cot, and cash contribution.
Is displaced with the Campbells to the shelter.
Moves out to a room with Ann-Marie and Xiomara.

characters.availability: Absent until chapter_11, then present through chapter_18.

state_transitions.change: Helps Tiana plan around the UF hold.
Relationship with Tiana becomes sexual.
Offers the Gainesville drive, but the search for Micah overrides it.

characters.availability: Present in chapters 06, 08, 17, and by phone in chapter_18.

timeline.story_start: June 2019: Tiana's graduation night in Little River, with a red 90-day code notice already on the garage apartment.

seasonal_track.season: Storm season into the next housing step

key_events.event: UF dorm deposit of $225 is due August 1 while the code notice starts the housing clock.
UF file goes on hold over the expired resident card and missing 2017 parent verification.
Yvette's hand collapses; Marcia loses the expected $5,800 draw.
Delroy admits he spent Tiana's renewal money on May rent.
Celia dies and Marcia loses her main job.
Delroy tears his shoulder at Palm Court demolition.
Claude's visit exposes the $400 debt and Devon remittances; Delroy leaves.
Flood makes the unit uninhabitable and sends the family to New Testament Baptist shelter.
UF grants late check-in until Monday 5 PM; Tiana misses it while helping find Micah; housing cancels at 5:03 PM.
The family leases the airport-area apartment and Tiana signs into Miami Dade Honors.

geography.primary_setting: Little River, Miami; later New Testament Baptist shelter and a smaller airport-area apartment.

key_locations.name: Campbell apartment
key_locations.details: Converted garage behind a stucco front house; illegal occupancy, thin walls, flood-prone.

key_locations.name: UF Gainesville campus
key_locations.details: Orientation, aid hold, and missed move-in path.

key_locations.name: Immigration-help office on Fifty-Fourth
key_locations.details: Miss Gumbs lays out the renewal and fee-waiver packet.

key_locations.name: Palm Court Apartments
key_locations.details: Old fourplex off Seventy-First where Delroy is injured.

key_locations.name: New Testament Baptist fellowship hall
key_locations.details: Crowded shelter with four cots and a folding church mattress.

key_locations.name: Airport-area apartment
key_locations.details: Behind a tire shop; water included, one parking space, first-Tuesday pest control, constant plane noise.

distances: Little River to Gainesville is an all-day northbound drive.
Little River to Coral Gables is a long bus commute.
The airport apartment is farther from the old neighborhood and under the flight path.

world_rules: Contemporary realistic Miami; no speculative elements.
Expired immigration paperwork can block aid, housing, and school movement.
Informal partner money and off-book labor give poor families no legal protection.
Code enforcement, schools, police, and public charity can all reduce crisis to paperwork fast.

power_structure.mechanism: rent leverage, compliance notices, barred reentry
informal debt tied to access to paid labor

objects.name: Red code-enforcement notice
objects.status: Starts the 90-day clock; edges bleed in the flood.

objects.name: UF housing packet and card
objects.status: Sets the $225 August 1 deposit; later water-warped and moot when housing cancels.

objects.name: Partner notebook and envelopes
objects.origin: Marcia's duplicate record of the hand
objects.status: The rescue plan dies with the hand; pages are water-damaged in chapter_16.

objects.name: Tiana's blue document folder
objects.origin: UF, school, immigration, and later lease papers
objects.status: Rescued from the flood; warped but usable.

objects.name: Cedar trunk
objects.status: Key originals are stripped out during the flood; the trunk itself is left behind.

objects.name: Remittance receipts envelope
objects.status: Found in chapter_13 and exposed in chapter_14.

objects.name: Marcia's nursing-school letter
objects.origin: Old Miami Dade practical-nursing admission packet
objects.status: Recovered during flood triage; proof of an abandoned path.

debts.amount: $1,150 rent plus late charges; extra security required for any extension past Friday
debts.status: Never stabilized before the flood ended the tenancy

debts.deadline: Informal collection tied to the demolition job
debts.status: Publicly called in at the apartment in chapter_14

debts.deadline: Needed to renew Tiana's resident-card status
debts.status: Packet assembled through Miss Gumbs in chapter_13

debts.deadline: August 1, then a late-check-in window through Monday 5 PM in chapter_17
debts.status: Lost when Tiana misses check-in and housing cancels at 5:03 PM

debts.deadline: Same night as Delroy's chapter_10 injury
debts.status: Unpaid; the family leaves with care instructions and a cheap sling only

debts.amount: About $140 for the comp reader and statistics access code
debts.deadline: Before first assignments after chapter_18 registration
debts.status: Unfunded when Tiana signs the local schedule

income_sources.amount: Roughly $60 to $90 on dock days plus small haircut cash
Regular hourly wages beginning in chapter_18
Probationary night-shift paycheck beginning in chapter_18

knowledge_state.knows: He spent Tiana's renewal money on May rent and hid that the renewal was never filed.
He has been sending money to Devon in Jamaica since June; the visible receipts total $270 across five transfers.

environmental_constants: Heat, damp, and mildew dominate indoor life until bus AC, storm wind, or the airport apartment changes the pressure.
Roosters, bass, buses, then low planes define the soundscape.
Bleach, frying oil, wet paper, liniment, and floodwater recur indoors.
Notices, receipts, folders, IDs, and schedules carry real leverage.
